---
title: "Applied Remote Sensing Research"
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

This section summarizes public, research-oriented remote sensing
application experience. Detailed operational engineering projects are now
described in the Experience section using desensitized and public-safe
language.

::: {.callout-note}
This page uses public-safe descriptions only. Internal source code,
client information, private implementation details, non-public system
screenshots, contract information, and non-public data are not disclosed.
:::

## Research-oriented Application Themes

### Satellite Data Preprocessing and Analysis-ready Products

I have worked with multi-source satellite observations in workflows that
connect raw imagery, preprocessing, quality control, and standardized
analysis-ready products. Public-safe examples include radiometric
calibration, atmospheric correction, geometric correction, image fusion,
format conversion, and batch product preparation for downstream
environmental monitoring tasks.

### Fire-related Environmental Observation

My applied remote sensing experience includes wildfire-relevant
environmental monitoring, thermal anomaly interpretation, time-series
surface-condition products, and regional raster statistics. This theme
connects naturally with my academic research on wildfire risk modeling,
fire ecology, and explainable GeoAI.

### Agricultural Monitoring and Crop Growth Assessment

I have contributed to agricultural remote sensing workflows involving
vegetation indicators, crop growth assessment, anomaly analysis, thematic
mapping, and county-level yield-estimation prototypes. The public
research value lies in linking satellite time-series information,
meteorological context, crop distribution, and statistical records for
interpretable agricultural monitoring.

### From Engineering Practice to Research Questions

These applied experiences help me formulate research questions that are
both technically grounded and environmentally meaningful: how to improve
the reliability of multi-source satellite products, how to integrate
remote sensing indicators with machine learning, and how to make spatial
models more interpretable for decision support.

More detailed professional summaries are provided in the
[Experience](../experience.qmd) section.
